content.text: *"Test everything; hold fast what is good."*{1 Thessalonians 5:21}{The method of truth tables is a decision procedure for propositional logic.}{Emil Post}

A **truth table** is a systematic way to display 
all possible truth values of a compound proposition based on the truth 
values of its components. Truth tables allow us to analyze logical 
statements exhaustively, leaving no possibility unconsidered.

For a compound proposition with $n$ distinct propositional variables, 
there are $2^n$ possible combinations of truth values. We list each 
combination as a row in the truth table.

content.title: Truth Table
content.content:
A **truth table** for a compound proposition lists every possible 
combination of truth values for its propositional variables, along with 
the resulting truth value of the compound proposition.

content.title: Truth tables for basic connectives
content.problem:
Here are the truth tables for the five basic connectives:

| $p$ | $\lnot p$ |
| $\T$ | $\F$ |
| $\F$ | $\T$ |

content.text:
For more complex propositions, we build the truth table step by step, 
computing intermediate columns for subexpressions.

content.title: Truth table for a compound proposition
content.problem: Construct the truth table for $(p \lor q) \limplies \lnot p$.
content.solution:
We need columns for $p$, $q$, $p \lor q$, $\lnot p$, and finally the 
implication:

| $p$ | $q$ | $p \lor q$ | $\lnot p$ | $(p \lor q) \limplies \lnot p$ |
| $\T$ | $\T$ | $\T$ | $\F$ | $\F$ |
| $\T$ | $\F$ | $\T$ | $\F$ | $\F$ |
| $\F$ | $\T$ | $\T$ | $\T$ | $\T$ |
| $\F$ | $\F$ | $\F$ | $\T$ | $\T$ |

The compound proposition is true only when $p$ is false.

content.title: Tautology and Contradiction
content.content:
A **tautology** is a compound proposition that is 
true for all possible truth values of its components.

A **contradiction** is a compound proposition 
that is false for all possible truth values of its components.

A **contingency** is a proposition that is neither 
a tautology nor a contradiction.

content.title: Tautology: Law of Excluded Middle
content.problem: Show that $p \lor \lnot p$ is a tautology.
content.solution:
| $p$ | $\lnot p$ | $p \lor \lnot p$ |
| $\T$ | $\F$ | $\T$ |
| $\F$ | $\T$ | $\T$ |

The final column is all $\T$'s, confirming this is a tautology.

content.title: Contradiction: Law of Non-Contradiction
content.problem: Show that $p \land \lnot p$ is a contradiction.
content.solution:
| $p$ | $\lnot p$ | $p \land \lnot p$ |
| $\T$ | $\F$ | $\F$ |
| $\F$ | $\T$ | $\F$ |

The final column is all $\F$'s, confirming this is a contradiction.

marginNotes.text:
The simple believe anything, but the 
prudent give thought to their steps.
